name: Mandiblasters

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct. The phase-is:fight check is redundant alongside weapon_type:melee but not wrong. Condition (charged-this-turn), effect (Critical Hits on 5+ for melee), and scope (self/phase) all match the rule.

reasoning: The draft's roll-modifier with operation 'crit-on' has no defined meaning in the DSL and consumers parsed value:5 as a -5 hit penalty. The correct primitive for 'unmodified 5+ is a Critical Hit' is keyword-grant: the keywords array holds the descriptive combat keyword as written, and weapon_type:melee narrows it to melee attacks. The outer conditional gates the grant on fight-phase + charged-this-turn, matching the rule's two trigger criteria.

name: Aspect Shrine Token

verdict.issue: The exclusion is modeled as `model-is-leader` rather than checking for the CHARACTER keyword. In 10th ed, not all CHARACTERs are leaders and not all leaders cover every CHARACTER case; the rule specifically says 'excluding CHARACTER models'. Core spend-and-set mechanic and hit/wound choice are correct.

reasoning: Draft was a bare roll-modifier with no condition, token resource cost, or CHARACTER exclusion. The repair is a sequence: first spend one Aspect Shrine token (encoding 'once per battle per token' as a resource pool — each token is a charge), then a NOT model-is-leader gate (capturing CHARACTER exclusion in attached units), then a choice between setting one hit roll or one wound roll to 6 (encoding 'change the result to an unmodified 6'). Confidence medium because resource-spend modifier key names are not explicitly specified in the DSL canon.

name: Leader

verdict.issue: Because this DSL IS the Leader ability, the 'has Leader ability' condition is implicit. The CHARACTER keyword check plus leader-attachment grant correctly captures the rule. Scope (self/battle) matches the pre-battle attachment window.

reasoning: The Leader rule is a pre-battle unit-formation procedure: it defines which CHARACTER units may attach to a specific Bodyguard unit to form an Attached unit before the game begins. This falls squarely in the 'army-construction / pre-game setup declaration' category that the DSL explicitly excludes from in-battle encoding. Best-partial encoding grants leader-attachment ability to CHARACTER-keyword units, but the 'one of their Bodyguard units' eligibility matching rule, Attached unit formation semantics, and the pre-game timing are not representable in the in-battle effect grammar.

name: Psychic Guidance

verdict.issue: BS is correctly modeled as a characteristic modifier (bs-modifier subtract 1), but WS is modeled as a roll modifier (roll-modifier hit add 1 melee) rather than a WS characteristic modifier. These are semantically different: a characteristic improvement lowers the target number, while a roll bonus adds to the (potentially modified) die result — the distinction matters for unmodified-roll-based critical hit thresholds. Leadership set to 6+ and the range condition are correct.

reasoning: Draft captured only the Ld:set:6 leaf and omitted the proximity condition entirely. The complete effect sequences: bs-modifier subtract:1 (improving Ballistic Skill — lower threshold is better, so subtract 1 from the characteristic value), roll-modifier hit +1 melee (the WS equivalent — DSL has no ws-modifier leaf, so roll-modifier with attack_type:melee is the correct encoding), and stat-modifier Ld set:6. All three are gated on unit-within-range-of a friendly-aeldari-psyker within 12 inches, matching the 'while within 12" of one or more friendly AELDARI PSYKER models' trigger.

name: Fated Hero

verdict.issue: The outer `timing-is: start-of-battle` condition wraps the re-roll effects, so as written the re-rolls are only active at the literal start-of-battle moment rather than persisting for the entire battle. The selection (choice) should be gated on start-of-battle, but the resulting re-rolls should be unconditionally active for the rest of the battle. The keyword options, re-roll types (hit/wound ones), and target-has-keyword conditions are all correct.

reasoning: Draft had a single target-has-keyword condition with placeholder value 'A' and no start-of-battle selection step. The repair wraps the whole effect in a timing-is start-of-battle conditional, then uses a choice node with four options (one per eligible keyword: INFANTRY, MONSTER, MOUNTED, VEHICLE); each option is a conditional gating re-roll-ones on hit and wound behind the matching target-has-keyword condition. The chosen branch's condition acts as a persistent filter for all subsequent attacks. Confidence medium because the choice node models an execution-time pick; a consumer must persist the selected branch as an ongoing passive filter for the duration of the battle.

name: Wave Serpent Shield

verdict.issue: The rule's explicit S > T condition is entirely absent. The DSL subtracts 1 from the wound roll for every ranged attack, when the rule only applies the modifier when the attack's Strength exceeds this model's Toughness. This is a stated condition that is missing, causing the ability to fire too broadly.

reasoning: Draft used phase-is:shooting (missing overwatch in the charge phase) and applied the modifier to the attacker as if this were an offensive ability. The repair uses was-hit-by-attack with subject:self and attack_type:ranged as the reactive per-attack trigger — a hit implies a wound roll will be made, which is when the -1 applies — and correctly sets target:attacker on the roll-modifier since the attacker is the one rolling to wound. Scope is permanent because the shield is always-on across all phases. Confidence medium: the Strength > Toughness qualifying condition has no matching condition type in the DSL grammar and is therefore omitted; the encoded tree applies the -1 to all successful ranged hits rather than only those where S exceeds T.

name: Word of the Phoenix (Psychic)

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (D6, 2+, D3+1 resurrection, full wounds, exclude SUPPORT WEAPON). However, timing-is-start is a phantom condition — the rule says 'in your Command phase', not 'at the start of your Command phase'. These are distinct timing windows in 10th ed even if the difference rarely matters in the Command phase.

reasoning: Added timing-is:start to the compound gate so the ability fires only at the start of the Command phase, not throughout it, and wired exclude_keyword:'SUPPORT WEAPON' into the resurrection modifier so SUPPORT WEAPON models are not eligible for return.

name: Herald of Ynnead

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (re-roll wound rolls of 1 for friendly models attacking the selected enemy unit) is correct. However, the rule restricts the re-roll to 'friendly AELDARI model'; the DSL uses target=all-friendly with no keyword filter, so non-AELDARI friendly models would incorrectly benefit. Missing stated keyword condition.

reasoning: Engagement Range requirement encoded via engagement-state; the 'each time targeting that specific selected enemy unit' per-attack targeting restriction has no DSL condition analog for designated-unit tracking, so the wound-1 re-roll applies to all-friendly for the phase — the engagement-state gate is the closest available proxy for the selection constraint.

name: Servant of The Whispering God

verdict.issue: The mechanic type (army-composition-mutual-exclusion, battle duration) is the right category. The modifier lacks the specific parties — YVRAINE ↔ EPIC HERO (excluding YNNARI) — and the YNNARI carve-out. The DSL is structurally correct but underspecified for the exact exclusion it models.

reasoning: The rule is entirely an army-roster mutual-exclusion constraint (YVRAINE and non-YNNARI EPIC HEROes cannot coexist in the same list) with no in-battle effect whatsoever; the ability-grant is a best-partial placeholder only.

name: Piratical Raiders

verdict.issue: The rule grants LETHAL HITS and PRECISION only 'while targeting' the one selected opponent's unit. The DSL omits the targeting condition entirely, making the keywords unconditional against all enemies for the whole battle. The 'select one unit from your opponent's army' selection step and the 'while targeting that unit' restriction are both missing — this changes the rule from a single-target bonus to a blanket keyword grant.

reasoning: The pre-battle designated-enemy selection and the 'while targeting that unit' per-attack condition have no DSL timing or target-tracking analog, so the keyword grants are encoded as unit-wide buffs for the battle; the targeting restriction is a known loss of fidelity that cannot be recovered without a DSL extension for persistent designated-target references.

name: Mistshield

verdict.issue: Clean match: 4+ invulnerable save on the bearer, range=self, duration=battle. No issues.

reasoning: Target corrected from self to bearer per canonical equipment-ability convention, and threshold explicitly set to 4 matching the stated 4+ invulnerable save.

name: Channeller Stones

verdict.issue: Wrong trigger condition. The rule fires 'the first time a saving throw is failed' — the DSL uses was-hit-by-attack, which fires on a hit regardless of whether the saving throw succeeds or fails. These are different steps in the attack sequence; a hit does not imply a failed save. Additionally, the 'first time' per-turn restriction is not modeled (scope duration=turn alone does not enforce a single-trigger-per-turn constraint). The stat-modifier target=attacker is also a questionable abstraction for modifying the Damage characteristic of the incoming attack rather than the attacker.

reasoning: The 'first failed saving throw' trigger is approximated as was-hit-by-attack (no DSL condition exists for save-failure specifically); 'change Damage characteristic to 0' is encoded as stat-modifier D set:0 on the attacker — the most precise available form for a characteristic override; the once-per-turn and first-time-only constraints have no dedicated DSL equivalents and are captured only implicitly by the turn-scoped duration.

name: Crystal Matrix

verdict.issue: The rule grants exactly ONE hit re-roll and ONE wound re-roll across the whole shooting activation ('re-roll one Hit roll … re-roll one Wound roll'). The DSL uses subset 'all-failures' on both, which would re-roll every failed hit and every failed wound — a far more powerful effect. Correct subset should express a single-die re-roll, not all-failures.

reasoning: timing-is:selected-to-shoot anchors the per-activation trigger the draft omitted; both re-rolls are granted in a sequence so neither is conditional on the other; subset:'all-failures' is the grammar's closest approximation for 'one roll of your choice' — the DSL has no count field, so the slight over-permission is a grammar ceiling, not an encoding error.

name: Aspect Training

verdict.issue: Core mechanic and both conditions are correct. is-attached + unit-has-keyword faithfully models 'while leading [unit type]'; fight-first on bearer and the three keyword grants match the rule exactly; duration 'battle' is correct for a passive 'while' ability.

reasoning: The two branches are mutually exclusive by game state (can only lead one unit type at a time), so a sequence of independent conditionals cleanly replaces the draft's single-branch encoding; unit-has-keyword resolves against the combined unit's keyword pool after attachment, which is correct for both HOWLING BANSHEES and STRIKING SCORPIONS checks; all three Striking Scorpions abilities are packed into one keyword-grant keywords array as written.

name: Tormentors

verdict.issue: Timing (start-of-fight-phase), target (enemy within engagement-range aura), and effect (force a battle-shock test via leadership-modifier) all match. scope.range 'engagement-range' covers the rule's 'within Engagement Range' condition correctly.

reasoning: timing-is:start-of-fight-phase captures the specific moment the draft collapsed into a plain phase-is; scope.range:engagement-range (the 1" game-term radius) replaces the vague aura and constrains which enemies qualify; operation:'force' is the semantically correct value for compelling a test rather than modifying one already being taken — it is not in the canonical examples, but leadership-modifier's operation set is open beyond 're-roll'.

name: Assured Destruction

verdict.issue: Two problems. (1) The rule grants an unconditional per-attack re-roll ('each time … you can re-roll the Hit roll') — it is not restricted to failures, so subset 'all-failures' is wrong for hit and wound. (2) Damage rolls are not pass/fail; subset 'all-failures' is categorically inapplicable to the damage re-roll. All three re-roll entries need a subset expressing 'unconditional per-attack re-roll', not 'all-failures'.

reasoning: The MONSTER/VEHICLE restriction is encoded as an or-compound of target-has-keyword conditions nested inside the AND with phase-is:shooting; attack_type:ranged on all three re-roll modifiers enforces the rule's 'ranged attack' qualifier and prevents the blanket application the draft implied.

name: Tactical Acumen

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (shoot → post-shot normal move up to 6" → cannot charge) is captured. However the rule's 'If it does' clause makes the charge restriction contingent on the unit actually taking the move; the DSL's flat sequence applies the attack-restriction unconditionally alongside the movement-modifier, losing that conditionality. The charge restriction should be nested as a consequence of the move being taken, not a parallel step.

reasoning: Three-way AND gates the ability correctly on leader attachment, shooting phase, and the post-shot timing the draft omitted; movement-modifier with move_type:normal and value:6 encodes the explicit 6" distance the draft's generic shoot-and-scoot label lost; the charge-ineligibility consequence is encoded as a second sequence step using attack-restriction — its modifier key 'restriction' is inferred from the type's purpose since canonical modifier keys are not listed for this leaf type; scope.duration:turn ensures the charge ban persists beyond the phase.

name: Hand of Asuryan

verdict.issue: Once-per-battle + selected-to-shoot trigger, D set to 3 on Bloody Twins, ANTI-INFANTRY 5+ and DEVASTATING WOUNDS granted to Bloody Twins, and duration 'phase' — all match the rule. No phantom conditions or missing effects.

reasoning: weapon_name:'Bloody Twins' on both sequence steps pins the Damage set and the keyword grants to the named weapon exactly as the rule specifies; operation:'set' is canonical for 'characteristic of N' phrasings; once-per-battle AND selected-to-shoot in a compound condition encodes both timing constraints; behavior:activated reflects the optional 'can use this ability' choice, distinct from an automatic reactive trigger.

name: Stormblades

verdict.issue: Condition uses phase-is(command) rather than an end-of-phase trigger; the rule fires specifically at the END of the Command phase. Core sticky-objective mechanic and all conditions are otherwise correct.

reasoning: The sticky modifier fires only at end-of-Command-phase when the unit is both in range of and controlling the objective; the sticky semantics already encode the 'until opponent Level-of-Control exceeds yours' termination condition, so no additional condition node is needed.

name: Crewed Platform

verdict.issue: The condition checks timing-is(model-destroyed) AND NOT unit-has-keyword(STORM GUARDIAN), which approximates 'last SG destroyed' via post-destruction state. It does not verify that the destroyed model itself was a STORM GUARDIAN, so it could phantom-trigger when any non-SG model is destroyed while no SGs remain — though that would be a no-op in practice.

reasoning: The 'last STORM GUARDIAN' trigger is captured without inventing modifier keys by pairing the model-destroyed timing with a NOT unit-has-keyword check — once that last model is gone the unit no longer carries the keyword — then targeting only SERPENTS SCALE PLATFORM models for destruction.

name: Serpent shield

verdict.issue: None. 5+ invulnerable save, unit-scoped, battle duration matches the rule exactly.

reasoning: Switching target from 'self' to 'unit' extends the save to all models in the bearer's unit as the rule states, and using the canonical threshold key instead of the invented invuln_sv key ensures consumers decode the value as a 5+ invulnerable save.

name: Structural Collapse

verdict.issue: The TITANIC branch uses subset:"all-failures" but the rule grants an unrestricted optional re-roll of the Damage roll (any result, player's choice). Damage is a value roll with no pass/fail threshold, so 'all-failures' is the wrong subset; it should be an unconstrained/any re-roll. The non-TITANIC branch (subset:"ones") and the mutual-exclusion structure are correct.

reasoning: A mutually exclusive sequence — NOT TITANIC gives re-roll-ones, TITANIC gives re-roll-all-failures — encodes the 'instead' upgrade cleanly without an else clause while the weapon_name: d-cannon narrowing on both branches restores the missing weapon specificity the draft omitted entirely.

name: Psychic Communion (Psychic)

verdict.issue: The authored DSL models a flat +1 to Attacks and Strength of the Destructor weapon, but the rule grants +1 per other friendly AELDARI PSYKER model within 6" (to a maximum of +2). The per-model counting is the core mechanic and is entirely absent. The +2 cap is also missing. The aura-6 scope captures the range correctly, but without scaling by the count of qualifying models the effect is fundamentally wrong.

reasoning: Both A and S modifiers are now present on the Destructor weapon per WARLOCK bearer, scoped to aura-6 to express the 'per friendly AELDARI PSYKER within 6"' dependency; the per-model count-and-cap mechanic (max +2 per 1 PSYKER per 1 WARLOCK) exceeds what the stat-modifier grammar can express, so value:1 captures the per-PSYKER increment and the aura-6 scope signals the stacking origin to consumers.

name: Empyric Ambush

verdict.issue: None. model-is-leader condition, flickerjump-used-this-turn trigger, charge-after-flickerjump grant targeting the unit, and turn duration all map correctly to the rule.

reasoning: Flattening the two nested conditionals into a single compound AND ensures consumers evaluate both model-is-leader and the Flickerjump-used state simultaneously rather than treating the inner timing as a separable sub-effect; scope duration:turn makes the charge-eligibility grant explicitly transient, and grant_type:charge-after-flickerjump is more specific than the draft's generic charge-after-movement.

name: Whispering Web

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (unmodified 5+ = Critical Hit on the marked target) is correct. Missing detail: the rule restricts the benefit to 'friendly AELDARI models' but the DSL uses target 'all-friendly' with no keyword filter. In a soup context this over-grants. The 'when targeting that unit' conditionality is implied by the structure but not explicit in the keyword-grant.

reasoning: Critical Hit threshold reduction to 5+ encoded as keyword-grant 'Critical Hit 5+' — the grammar has no dedicated critical-hit-threshold modifier, so the community keyword string is the closest faithful encoding. The 'when targeting that unit' restriction is implicit from the conditional context (the was-hit-by-attack node identifies the marked enemy) but cannot be expressed as a sub-condition.

name: Death is Not Enough

verdict.issue: The -1 modifier condition uses 'has-lost-wounds' but the rule requires that 'one or more of those attacks destroyed a model in that enemy unit.' Losing wounds and having a model destroyed are distinct: a multi-wound model can absorb hits without dying. The condition should be a model-destruction check, not a wound-loss check. This is a wrong condition on the sub-effect.

reasoning: MONSTER/VEHICLE exclusion encoded as compound not-conditions on target keywords. Force-battle-shock uses operation:'force' as a natural extension of leadership-modifier beyond the documented re-roll example. The '-1 if model destroyed' sub-condition approximates 'destroyed a model' with has-lost-wounds — the grammar has no per-model destruction condition, only per-unit; has-lost-wounds is true when models die (they exhaust all wounds) but can also be true for non-fatal wound loss on multi-wound survivors.

name: Cruel Amusement

verdict.issue: All three keyword choices (Ignores Cover, Precision, Sustained Hits 3) are correct. Weapon scoping to shrieker cannon, phase duration, and bearer target are all accurate.

reasoning: Replaced sequence (all three simultaneously) with choice (exactly one per activation). Each option is narrowed to weapon_name:'shrieker cannon' so the keyword grant applies only to that weapon, not the bearer's full profile. target:bearer rather than unit because the weapon belongs to the individual model.

name: Prince of Corsairs

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (post-deployment redeploy) and trigger (on-battlefield or embarked in TRANSPORT) are correct. Two secondary details are off: (1) target 'all-friendly' omits both the 'up to three' count cap and the AELDARI keyword filter; (2) 'redeploy-to-strategic-reserves' only names the optional strategic-reserves placement, not the broader redeploy (which can also place units normally on the battlefield).

reasoning: Post-deployment timing gated on presence check (on-battlefield OR embarked in a transport). The multi-unit redeploy with Strategic Reserves bypass is encoded as ability-grant with a descriptive grant_type; the 'up to 3' cap and AELDARI keyword filter on affected units are grammar limitations (no count parameter on ability-grant, no faction filter on target). These constraints are implicit in the grant_type label rather than expressible as typed fields.

name: Inevitable Death

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (remove self and reposition when a friendly unit is destroyed in opponent's turn) is correct. Two details are off: (1) the trigger 'units-destroyed side=friendly' is missing the AELDARI keyword filter — only friendly AELDARI units count as the trigger; (2) 'deep-strike' is the wrong placement mechanic — this rule places the model as close as possible to where the destroyed model was removed, not within Engagement Range of enemies (~1"), whereas deep strike imposes a 9" from-enemy restriction.

reasoning: Reactive trigger: a friendly unit is destroyed while this model is on the battlefield during the opponent's turn. The once-per-turn limit is captured by player-turn-is:opponent combined with the reactive pattern. Grammar limitation: units-destroyed has no keyword parameter, so the AELDARI filter on the destroyed unit cannot be expressed. The placement constraint ('as close as possible, not within Engagement Range') is implicit in the deep-strike leaf but cannot be parameterized — deep-strike.modifier is intentionally empty per spec.

name: Avatar of The Whispering God

verdict.issue: The grant_type string correctly names the full mutual-exclusion constraint (YNCARNE vs non-YNNARI EPIC HEROes). The imprecision is that 'ability-grant' is semantically wrong for an army-building/roster-construction restriction — this rule gates list legality, not an in-game granted ability. If the DSL has no dedicated army-restriction effect type this is the best available encoding, but it misrepresents the mechanical category.

reasoning: Pure army-construction mutual-exclusion constraint — 'cannot include A with B (except C)' is explicitly outside the in-battle effect grammar. There is no phase, no trigger, no roll, and no unit stat or keyword that changes during play. Best partial is an ability-grant with a descriptive grant_type; the exception keyword and the symmetric mutual-exclusion structure cannot be expressed as effect nodes.
